page: Amazon Web Services m6idn.metal is a bare-metal, general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with hyper-threading, operating at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 512 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. For local storage, the instance includes 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it delivers a high network baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with the server achieving top-tier performance in Passmark CPU and extended instruction tests. Single-core performance sits in the average tier, but multi-threaded workloads like bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core tests show strong results. The hardware excels in memory bandwidth, registering strong read and write speeds for large uncached blocks. Additionally, it demonstrates top-tier performance in ray tracing and image processing, alongside strong throughput for static web serving and Redis SET operations.

While the server delivers strong performance for prompt processing on small and medium LLM models, text generation for large 70B models is weak, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration. Given the high resource density of 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 7.6 TB of local NVMe storage, this bare-metal instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access without hypervisor overhead. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.metal is a bare-metal general-purpose server configured with 128 vCPUs, 64 physical cores, and 512 GB of DDR4 memory. Powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, it integrates 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 200 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier results in Passmark CPU and extended instruction tests. While single-core performance is average and large-scale LLM text generation is limited by the absence of a GPU, the server excels in multi-threaded execution, image processing, and database operations. This bare-metal configuration is highly suited for high-throughput databases, enterprise applications, static web serving, and intensive data processing workloads requiring direct hardware access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6idn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for databases and multi-threaded workloads.

tagline: A high-capacity bare-metal instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, massive NVMe storage, and high-speed network throughput for enterprise workloads.
